data.content:
### Create Partition

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/partitions

Allows for explicit creation of a partition.

```APIDOC
## POST /partitions

### Description
Creates a new partition explicitly. Partitions can also be created implicitly when documents, connections, or instructions are added to them.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/partitions

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **partition** (string) - Optional - The name of the partition to create. If omitted, a default name may be used.

#### Request Body
- **name** (string) - Required - The name for the new partition. Must be lowercase alphanumeric and may include `_` and `-`.
- **limits** (object) - Optional - Configuration for partition limits (e.g., document count, storage size).
  - **max_documents** (integer) - Optional - Maximum number of documents allowed in this partition.
  - **max_storage_bytes** (integer) - Optional - Maximum storage size in bytes allowed for this partition.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "name": "my_new_partition",
  "limits": {
    "max_documents": 1000,
    "max_storage_bytes": 1073741824
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (201 Created)
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier for the created partition.
- **name** (string) - The name of the created partition.
- **created_at** (string) - Timestamp when the partition was created.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "part-1234abcd",
  "name": "my_new_partition",
  "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Endpoints Supporting Partition Scoping

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/partitions

A list of endpoints that support partition scoping via the `partition` header or SDK parameter.

```APIDOC
## Endpoints that support partition scoping

- Get Document
- Delete Document
- Update Document File
- Update Document Raw
- Patch Document Metadata
- Get Document Chunks
- Get Document Chunk
- Get Document Summary
- Get Instruction Extracted Entities
- Get Document Extracted Entities
```

--------------------------------

### Explicitly Creating Partitions

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/partitions

Details on how to explicitly create partitions, which can be useful for pre-defining limits.

```APIDOC
## Managing Partitions

### Explicitly Creating Partitions
While partitions can be created implicitly through various operations like creating a document, they can also be explicitly created using [Create Partition](https://docs.ragie.ai/reference/create_partition_partitions_post). This can be useful if creating a partition with limits defined is required before documents are created in it.
```

--------------------------------

### Working with Partitions

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/partitions

This section covers how partitions are handled implicitly and explicitly when interacting with Ragie AI resources.

```APIDOC
## Working with Partitions

### Creating Partitions
A Partition is automatically created anytime a document, connection or instruction is created in it.

### Documents and Partitions
Documents can be created in a partition by providing an optional `partition` string when creating them. If a partition is omitted the document will be created in the "default" partition.

### Retrievals
The optional `partition` parameter can be provided when doing retrievals. When present the retrieval will be scoped to that Partition. Fine grained scoping via metadata filters may be combined with partition scoping.

### Connections and Partitions
Connections can be created in a partition by providing an optional `partition` string when creating them. If a partition is omitted, documents managed by the partition will be in the "default" partition.

### Entity Extraction Instructions and Partitions
Instructions can be created in a partition by providing an optional `partition` string when creating them. If a partition is provided, the instruction will attempt to extract entities from documents in all partitions. If a partition is provided, the instruction will only execute on documents in that partition.

### Partitions Handling for Other Endpoints
Many other endpoints support scoping the request to a partition using the `partition` HTTP header or a parameter with that same name in the Ragie SDKs. If `partition` is omitted, requests will be scoped to the "default" partition. Accounts created prior to 1/9/2025 will have requests scoped to all partitions unless they opt-in to stricter enforcement. It is strongly encouraged to explicitly set `partition` on requests that support it.
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Multi-Tenant Partitions with Ragie SDK

Source: https://context7.com/context7/ragie_ai/llms.txt

Demonstrates creating, uploading to, retrieving from, checking usage, and deleting partitions for customer data isolation. Requires an API key for authentication. Handles document storage, retrieval, and usage limits per partition.

```typescript
import { Ragie } from "ragie";

const ragie = new Ragie({ auth: "YOUR_API_KEY" });

// Create partition for tenant
const partition = await ragie.partitions.create({
  partitionId: "org_acme_corp",
  limits: {
    pagesProcessedLimitMonthly: 10000,
    pagesHostedLimitMonthly: 50000
  }
});

// Upload document to partition
const document = await ragie.documents.create({
  file: fileBlob,
  name: "Acme Corp Data",
  partition: "org_acme_corp",
  metadata: {
    tenant_id: "acme_corp",
    uploaded_by: "user_123"
  }
});

// Retrieve within partition scope
const results = await ragie.retrievals.retrieve({
  query: "customer contracts",
  partition: "org_acme_corp",
  filter: {
    tenant_id: "acme_corp"
  }
});

// Check partition usage
const usage = await ragie.partitions.get({
  partitionId: "org_acme_corp"
});

console.log(`Pages processed: ${usage.pagesProcessedMonthly}`);
console.log(`Pages hosted: ${usage.pagesHostedMonthly}`);
console.log(`Document count: ${usage.documentCount}`);

if (usage.limitExceededAt) {
  console.warn(`Partition limited since: ${usage.limitExceededAt}`);
}

// Delete partition and all its data
await ragie.partitions.delete({ partitionId: "org_acme_corp" });
```

--------------------------------

### Clear Documents with Ragie CLI

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-cli

Removes documents from Ragie using the CLI. Supports optional dry-run to preview changes and specifying a partition.

```bash
ragie clear [--dry-run] [--partition your-partition]
```

--------------------------------

### Import Readme.io Data (Ragie CLI)

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-cli

Imports data from a Readme.io zip archive using the Ragie CLI. Supports optional dry-run, delay, and partition settings.

```bash
ragie import readmeio path/to/readme.zip [--dry-run] [--delay 2.0] [--partition your-partition]
```

--------------------------------

### Import Files from Directory (Ragie CLI)

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-cli

Imports all non-empty files from a specified directory recursively using the Ragie CLI. Includes metadata like source_type, path, extension, size, and mod_time. Supports optional dry-run, delay, and partition settings.

```bash
ragie import files path/to/directory [--dry-run] [--delay 2.0] [--partition your-partition]
```

--------------------------------

### Embedded Dropbox Connector - Configuration Options

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/dropbox-connection

This JSON example shows the configuration body for an embedded Dropbox connector. It includes parameters like redirect_uri, partition, source_type, and importantly, 'account_mode' to specify either a 'team' or 'user' account.

```json
{
  "redirect_uri": "https://secure.ragie.ai/connectors",
  "partition": "dropboxfiles",
  "source_type": "dropbox",
  "metadata": {},
  "mode": "hi_res",
  "theme": "system",
  "page_limit": 1000,
  "config": {
    "account_mode": "team"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Import Files from ZIP Archive (Ragie CLI)

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-cli

Imports files contained within a ZIP archive without extracting them using the Ragie CLI. Preserves metadata such as source_type, path, size, compressed_size, mod_time, and zip_source. Supports optional dry-run, delay, and partition settings.

```bash
ragie import zip path/to/archive.zip [--dry-run] [--delay 2.0] [--partition your-partition]
```

--------------------------------

### Import WordPress Data (Ragie CLI)

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-cli

Imports data from a WordPress XML export file using the Ragie CLI. Supports optional dry-run, delay, and partition settings.

```bash
ragie import wordpress path/to/wordpress.xml [--dry-run] [--delay 2.0] [--partition your-partition]
```

--------------------------------

### Webhook Event Handling

Source: https://context7.com/context7/ragie_ai/llms.txt

Register your application endpoints to receive real-time notifications from Ragie AI for various events, including document processing status, connection synchronization, entity extraction, and partition limit warnings.

```APIDOC
## POST /webhooks/ragie

### Description
Endpoint to receive webhook events from Ragie AI. It verifies the signature and processes incoming events.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/webhooks/ragie

### Headers
- **x-signature** (string) - Required - The signature of the payload for verification.

### Request Body
Received as raw JSON.

### Request Example (Event Payload)
```json
{
  "nonce": "unique_event_id_123",
  "type": "document_status_updated",
  "document_id": "doc_abc456",
  "status": "ready"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Indicates the status of the webhook processing (e.g., "received", "already processed").

#### Error Response (401)
- **error** (string) - "Invalid signature" if the signature verification fails.

### Event Types
- **document_status_updated**: Notification when a document's processing status changes (e.g., "ready", "failed").
- **entity_extracted**: Notification when entities have been successfully extracted from a document.
- **connection_sync_finished**: Notification when a data source synchronization completes.
- **partition_limit_exceeded**: Warning when a partition limit is reached.
```

--------------------------------

### Initiate Google Drive OAuth Connection with Ragie API

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/white-label-with-embed-flow

This cURL command initiates the OAuth connection flow for Google Drive using a previously created authenticator. It specifies the redirect URI, partition, source type, and connection mode. The output is a JSON object containing a URL to start the authentication process.

```curl
curl --location 'https://api.ragie.ai/connections/oauth' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer <your_key>' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data '{ 
  "redirect_uri": "<Your redirect url>",
  "partition": "google_files",
  "source_type": "google_drive",
  "metadata": {}, 
  "mode": "hi_res",
  "theme": "system",
  "page_limit": 100,
  "authenticator_id": "b5e77fb7-026c-408f-837a-2bf3b4ba0c6b"
}'
```

--------------------------------

### Update Connection

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/managing-connections

Modifies the `partition_strategy` or `metadata` of an existing connection. Changes are applied during the next sync.

```APIDOC
## PUT /connections/{connection_id}

### Description
Update a connection's `partition_strategy` or `metadata`. The connection's documents will be updated to reflect these changes on their next sync.

### Method
PUT

### Endpoint
https://api.ragie.ai/connections/{connection_id}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **connection_id** (string) - Required - The unique identifier for the connection.

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **partition_strategy** (string) - Optional - The strategy to use for partitioning documents (e.g., `hi_res`).
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Key-value pairs for additional metadata.

### Request Example
```bash
curl https://api.ragie.ai/connections/{connection_id} \
  --request PUT \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $RAGIE_API_KEY" \
  --json '{ "partition_strategy": "hi_res",  "metadata": {"source": "google_drive"}}'
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message** (string) - Confirmation message.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Connection updated successfully."
}
```

--------------------------------

### Update Connection API - Ragie.AI

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/managing-connections

Updates a connection's `partition_strategy` or `metadata`. Changes are reflected on the next sync. This requires the connection ID, the updated fields, and an API key.

```curl
curl https://api.ragie.ai/connections/{connection_id} \
  --request PUT \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $RAGIE_API_KEY" \
  --json '{ "partition_strategy": "hi_res",  "metadata": {"source": "google_drive"}}'
```

--------------------------------

### Filter Chunks with Complex Logic (JavaScript)

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/step-3-retrieve-chunks

Illustrates advanced filtering of retrieved chunks using logical operators like $or. This allows for more sophisticated data selection based on multiple metadata conditions, enabling precise retrieval for specific use cases. It requires the Ragie client to be initialized.

```javascript
const response = await ragie.retrievals.retrieve({
  query: "davos",
  filter: {
    $or: [
      { scope: { $eq: "tutorial" } },
      { scope: { $eq: "production" } },
    ],
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Enhance Retrieval Diversity with Summary Index

Source: https://context7.com/context7/ragie_ai/llms.txt

Illustrates using the summary index for retrieving diverse chunks across multiple documents. This prevents results from clustering in a single document. Requires an API key. Outputs the count of unique documents for standard and diverse retrievals.

```typescript
import { Ragie } from "ragie";

const ragie = new Ragie({ auth: "YOUR_API_KEY" });

// Standard retrieval (may return all chunks from one document)
const standardResults = await ragie.retrievals.retrieve({
  query: "product features",
  topK: 24
});

// Summary index retrieval (spreads across documents)
const diverseResults = await ragie.retrievals.retrieve({
  query: "product features",
  topK: 24,
  maxChunksPerDocument: 2  // Max 2 chunks per doc = ~12 documents
});

console.log(`Standard: ${countUniqueDocuments(standardResults)} documents`);
console.log(`Diverse: ${countUniqueDocuments(diverseResults)} documents`);

// Retrieve document summary directly
const summary = await ragie.documents.getSummary({
  documentId: "doc_id_here"
});

console.log(summary.text);  // LLM-generated summary of entire document
```

--------------------------------

### Create Google Drive Connection - cURL

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/whitelabel-connectors

This snippet shows how to create a Google Drive connection using cURL. It requires the authenticator ID obtained previously and a bearer token. The request body includes partitioning strategies, page limits, metadata, and connection-specific details like data IDs and refresh tokens.

```bash
curl --location 'https://ragie.ai/authenticators/963cad11-50d2-477b-94d7-d039beecf3db/connection' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer your_token' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data '{ \
    "partition_strategy": { \
        "static": "hi_res", \
        "audio": true, \
        "video": "audio_only" \
    }, \
    "partition": "string", \
    "page_limit": 1000, \
    "metadata": {}, \
    "connection": { \
        "provider": "google_drive", \
        "data": [ \
            { \
                "id": "id", 
                "name": "name", \
                "mime_type": "application/vnd.google-apps.folder" \
            } \
        ], \
        "email": "test", \
        "credentials": { \
            "refresh_token": "string" \
        } \
    } \
}'
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieval Endpoint with Summary Index

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/summary-index

This endpoint allows for advanced retrieval using the Summary Index feature. By specifying `max_chunks_per_document`, you can ensure that retrieved chunks span across multiple documents, improving the diversity of search results. The retrieval process involves retrieving top documents based on their summaries, then retrieving chunks from those documents, and optionally reranking the final results.

```APIDOC
## POST /websites/ragie_ai/retrievals

### Description
Retrieves relevant chunks of information based on a query, with support for the Summary Index feature to ensure diverse results across multiple documents.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/websites/ragie_ai/retrievals

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **rerank** (boolean) - Optional - If true, performs an LLM-based reranking of the results.

#### Request Body
- **query** (string) - Required - The search query.
- **top_k** (integer) - Required - The total number of chunks to retrieve.
- **filter** (object) - Optional - Filters for retrieval. Example: `{"collection": "sales"}`.
- **max_chunks_per_document** (integer) - Optional - The maximum number of chunks to retrieve from any single document. When set to a value greater than 0, it activates the Summary Index retrieval strategy.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "query": "what are the most requested features?",
  "top_k": 24,
  "filter": {
    "collection": "sales"
  },
  "rerank": false,
  "max_chunks_per_document": 2
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **chunks** (array) - A list of retrieved chunks, each containing relevant information.
  - **document_id** (string) - The ID of the document the chunk belongs to.
  - **chunk_id** (string) - The ID of the chunk.
  - **content** (string) - The content of the chunk.
  - **score** (float) - The similarity score of the chunk.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "chunks": [
    {
      "document_id": "doc123",
      "chunk_id": "chunk001",
      "content": "The feature for multi-document retrieval is highly requested.",
      "score": 0.95
    },
    {
      "document_id": "doc456",
      "chunk_id": "chunk002",
      "content": "Users want to see results from various sources.",
      "score": 0.92
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Entity Schema for Extracting Emails

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/entity-extraction

A JSON schema definition for extracting a list of email addresses. It specifies that the output should be an object containing an 'emails' property, which is an array of strings.

```json
"entity_schema": {
  "type": "object","properties": {"emails": {"type": "array","items": {"type": "string"}}}
}
```

--------------------------------

### RAG Retrieval Request with Max Chunks Per Document

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/summary-index

This JSON payload demonstrates a retrieval request to the Ragie API, specifying parameters for `top_k` chunks, filtering by collection, disabling reranking, and crucially, setting `max_chunks_per_document` to control the distribution of retrieved chunks across documents. This enables the Summary Index feature for more diverse document sourcing.

```json
{
  "query": "what are the most requested features?",
  "top_k": 24,
  "filter": {
    "collection": "sales"
  },
  "rerank": false,
  "max_chunks_per_document": 2
}
```

--------------------------------

### GET /documents/{document_id}/chunks

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/rate-limits

Retrieves all chunks of a specific document. Rate limits vary by plan.

```APIDOC
## GET /documents/{document_id}/chunks

### Description
Retrieves all chunks of a specific document.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/documents/{document_id}/chunks

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **document_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the document.

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
```json
{}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **chunks** (array) - A list of chunk objects.
  - **chunk_id** (string) - The ID of the chunk.
  - **content** (string) - The content of the chunk.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "chunks": [
    {
      "chunk_id": "chunk_abc",
      "content": "The first part of the document."
    }
  ]
}
```

### Rate Limits
- Developer: 10 / m
- Starter: 500 / m
- Pro: 1000 / m
- Enterprise: Custom
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Chunks

Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/step-3-retrieve-chunks

This endpoint allows you to retrieve raw chunks of data based on a query. It supports filtering results by metadata and offers an option to enable or disable reranking.

```APIDOC
## POST /retrieve

### Description
Retrieves raw chunks of data based on a query. Supports filtering by metadata and reranking for improved accuracy.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/retrieve

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **rerank** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to rerank the results. Defaults to true.

#### Request Body
- **query** (string) - Required - The search query to retrieve chunks.
- **filter** (object) - Optional - An object used to filter results by metadata. Can include operators like $eq, $in, $nin, $and, $or.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "query": "davos",
  "filter": {
    "scope": "tutorial"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **scored_chunks** (array) - An array of chunk objects, each containing text, score, document_id, and document_metadata.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "scored_chunks": [
    {
      "text": "...",
      "score": 0.4155921,
      "document_id": "21881de1-65f7-4816-b571-3ef69661c375",
      "document_metadata": { 
        "key": "value"
      }
    }
    // ... more chunks
  ]
}
```
```
